NASHVILLE-DAVIDSON COUNTY PLANNING DEPARTMENT
STAFF REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION

Case Number: PLN-2024-00180
Project: Antioch Town Center
Council District: 29
Requested by: Southeast Development Corp, applicant; Antioch Land Partners LLC, owner

Staff Reviewer: Michelle Torres, AICP
Date of Report: June 28, 2024

I. REQUEST

Rezone approximately 18 acres from CS (Commercial Services) and RS10 (Single-Family Residential, 10,000 SF lots) to SP-MU (Specific Plan - Mixed Use) to permit a master-planned mixed-use development.

The applicant proposes:
- Phase 1: 200 apartment units (4-story) and 30,000 SF of ground-floor retail
- Phase 2: 150 for-sale townhomes (3-story) and 10,000 SF office space
- Total estimated construction value: $95,000,000

Representative: Jason Adkins, attorney for applicant, of the law firm Bass Berry & Sims, 150 3rd Ave S, Suite 2800, Nashville TN 37201. Phone: 615-742-6200. Email jadkins@bassberry.com

II. EXISTING CONDITIONS

The site is located at the northeast corner of Antioch Pike and Bell Road, consisting of three parcels. Current improvements include a vacant grocery store (Parcel 1, ~45,000 SF), a surface parking lot (Parcel 2), and an undeveloped wooded tract (Parcel 3, RS10 zoning).

The property is served by existing water and sewer infrastructure. MTA Route 52 (Nolensville Road) provides transit access with a stop at the site frontage.

Neighboring uses include: single-family residential to the east and south, Antioch High School to the north, and Hickory Hollow area commercial to the west.

III. ANALYSIS

Community Plan: The Southeast Community Plan designates this area as a T4 Urban Mixed Use Neighborhood center, making the proposed SP-MU zoning consistent with the plan's vision for the area.

The proposed density of ~22 units/acre (Phase 1) is within the recommended range for T4-MU centers. Staff notes the inclusion of 15% workforce housing units (at 80% AMI), which the applicant has volunteered as part of the SP.

Project design team, per submitted materials:
- Site Planner: Kimley-Horn and Associates, Nashville office
  Contact: Brian Sexton, PE (TN PE #107223)
  Phone: 615-564-2701
  Note: Also serving as civil engineer for stormwater and utility design
- Architect: Centric Architecture, 200 4th Ave N, Suite 400, Nashville TN
  Contact: Ricardo Morales, AIA
  Phone: 615-742-8880
  Email: rmorales@centricarchitecture.com
- Landscape: Lose Design, 322 3rd Ave N, Nashville TN 37201
  Tom Loss was listed on preliminary plans but staff understands firm has since assigned Angela Wright, PLA to lead landscape design. No license number on file — applicant to provide at next submittal.
- Traffic Impact Study prepared by Neel-Schaffer Inc.
  Reviewed by Metro Public Works, found acceptable with conditions:
  1. Dedicated right-turn lane on Antioch Pike (NB approach)
  2. Signal timing optimization at Bell Rd intersection
  3. Pedestrian signal upgrades at 3 intersections

IV. RECOMMENDATION

Staff recommends APPROVAL WITH CONDITIONS of the proposed SP-MU rezoning.

Conditions:
1. Building height not to exceed 4 stories / 55 feet
2. Minimum 15% workforce housing at 80% AMI in Phase 1
3. Dedication of 30-foot ROW along Antioch Pike frontage
4. Traffic improvements per Neel-Schaffer TIS recommendations
5. Final landscape plan subject to MDHA Urban Design review

V. COMMISSION ACTION

To be heard at Planning Commission meeting of July 25, 2024.

_________________________________
Michelle Torres, AICP
Senior Planner
Nashville-Davidson County Planning Department
